summary: The network is a strong first draft (65 nodes, 94 edges) with the SL Perception Gate properly implemented (SL->D14; D14, D3 co-inhibit D53) and all canonical integrator hubs (D53, IPA1, TB1, MOC1, NGR5) appropriately wired. However, THREE other hormones (Gibberellin, Auxin, Cytokinin) bypass their receptors, producing Perception-Gate purity violations that will fail signalling-mutant and rescue tests. The ABA biosynthesis arm is absent (NCED1, OsHOX12 curated but excluded), the canonical axillary-meristem regulator TAB1/OsWUS is missing, and four newly-curated nodes (OsHOX12, NCED1, OsDRM1, OsHK3) flagged by Step 1.5 were not placed on any cascade. Tiller_Number (8 inputs) and TB1 (8 inputs) exceed the 7-input hard cap. Nine HIGH/MEDIUM structural suggestions; iterate required.

pathway_completeness.justification: SL biosynthesis + perception arms are complete (D27->D17/D10->MAX1->SL; D14+D3 co-inhibit D53; D53 represses IPA1 and MOC1). Nitrogen arm (NRT1.1B->NGR5; GATA8/LBD37->TCP19) is well-represented. BR (Brassinosteroid->BZR1/DLT) and clock (CCA1/PRR1) arms present. BUT: (a) ABA biosynthesis arm is a single summary edge T20->ABA with no NCED1/OsHOX12 intermediates despite both being curated (E091, E158, E157); (b) axillary-meristem initiation arm lacks TAB1/OsWUS despite E079, E080, E081 being curated; (c) auxin-signalling cascade has no OsTIR1 receptor or OsIAA/OsARF TFs despite E063-E065 being curated; (d) GA-signalling cascade lacks GID1 receptor despite E135/E136 curated. These are non-peripheral arms that ought to be modelled.

mechanistic_depth.justification: SL cascade is multi-step and layered (SL->D14->D53->{IPA1,MOC1,CKX9}). Clock is layered (Light/Photoperiod->CCA1/PRR1->TB1). But three major hormone arms use direct bypass edges that collapse mechanistic intermediates: Gibberellin->SLR1(-1) and Gibberellin->NGR5(-1) skip GID1 receptor; Cytokinin->TB1(-1) and Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA(+1) skip the HK4->AHP->RR21 transduction chain that is actually already in the network; Auxin->TB1(+1), Auxin->D10(+1), Auxin->D17(+1), Auxin->IPT(-1) skip OsTIR1-OsIAA-OsARF signalling entirely. NGR5's PRC2-mediated repression of SL biosynthesis genes (Wu 2020 Science) is not captured as D10/D27 edges from NGR5.

motif_coverage.justification: Perception Gate is applied cleanly for Strigolactone (only outgoing edge = D14) - excellent. But NOT applied for Gibberellin (2 direct outgoing: SLR1, NGR5), Cytokinin (3 direct outgoing despite HK4 being in-network), or Auxin (4 direct outgoing with no receptor in-network). Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance is done well for Cytokinin (IPT/LOG synthesise; CKX2/CKX9 degrade). Multi-Output Scaffold applied for IPA1 (->TB1, ->D53, ->PIN1B) and D53 (->IPA1, ->MOC1, ->CKX9). Feed-Forward motifs (Nitrogen->NGR5->TB1 and Nitrogen->TB1 direct) missing: currently N->NGR5 present but no direct N->TB1 (the Wu 2020 E113 edge is in curated but rejected).

cascade_balance.justification: Source-node percentage = 28/65 = 43.1%, within the softened 30-50% target range. No node has zero outputs except the phenotype. BUT two nodes violate the 7-input hard cap: TB1 has 8 inputs (IPA1+, NGR5-, CK-, Auxin+, CCA1+, BZR1-, T6P-, VIL2-) and Tiller_Number has 8 inputs (5 act / 3 inh). Several nodes are genuinely under-specified (1 input) where curated evidence has 3+ regulators: AHP (only HK4; E160 OsHK3->AHP rejected), GATA8 (input only from nothing - is_source=True but GATA8-H haplotype curation exists), RR21 (only AHP; biology has OsHK cascade redundancy).

composite_node_handling.justification: Partial. PIN1B is implicitly a PIN1a+1b composite (E011 OsPIN1b->IPA1 target and E058 OsPIN1b->TN target both land on PIN1B) but this is not documented in annotations. OsMAX1 is split into MAX1 (generic paralog family collapsed) + MAX1_1900 (Os1900 N-responsive) - documented. CKX family is partially composited: CKX2 and CKX9 separate, OsCKX4 (E044) and OsCKX11 (E046) excluded without documentation. OsHK family: only HK4 kept; OsHK3 (E161, E160) excluded. miR156/miR529 paralogs: only miR156 kept (E014 miR529->IPA1 excluded). These look like silent exclusions, not documented composites.

hub_completeness.justification: Major integrator hubs are well-regulated. TB1 (8 inputs from IPA1, NGR5, CK, Auxin, CCA1, BZR1, T6P, VIL2) covers canonical positive (IPA1, CCA1, Auxin) and negative (NGR5, CK, BZR1, T6P, VIL2) regulators. Missing: TB1->D14 feedback (E024) and TB1->MADS57 (E122) - but these would close a positive feedback loop (TRAP 1 risk) so defensible exclusion. IPA1 (7 inputs + 3 outputs) covers D53, miR156, IPI1, SHI1, RR21, CCA1, NGR5 - comprehensive. miR529 (E014) missing. D53 (4 inputs: D14-, D3-, IPA1+, Sucrose+) and MOC1 (4 inputs: D53-, TAD1-, TE-, SLR1+) are appropriate. Hubs overall solid - score lowered from 5 only because of miR529 and TB1 feedback defensibility documentation gap.

topology_hazards.justification: Multiple Perception-Gate purity violations = multiple TRAP 3 hazards. (a) Gibberellin has 2 direct outgoing (SLR1, NGR5) with no GID1 receptor - GA-GID1-SCF-SLR1 is a textbook SL-analog Perception Gate; (b) Cytokinin has 3 direct outgoing (TB1, HK4, RR_TYPEA) with HK4 in the network - Cytokinin->TB1 and Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA bypass the receptor; (c) Auxin has 4 direct outgoing (D10, D17, IPT, TB1) with no OsTIR1 in network - most egregious. Each of these will fail the corresponding receptor-KO + exogenous-hormone rescue tests in Step 4. No TRAP 1 positive feedback loops detected (network correctly broke TB1<->D14 feedback by excluding E024). No TRAP 2 redundancy issues. No self-loops.

evidence_quality.justification: Sampled 10 edges: all have DOI + title + authors + year + journal + evidence_sentence + claim (flat v2.0 schema). Mechanism strings are specific (e.g., E143 'SL-bound D14 directly binds D3 F-box protein to form D14-D3 complex', E140 'D53 contains EAR motifs and recruits TOPLESS/TPR co-repressors'). Some evidence sentences are 'abstract_read' rather than 'full_text_read' - about 20% of sampled edges. Edge N090 'OsGATA8 promotes tiller formation by repressing OsTCP19' is well-sourced (Wang 2024 Nat Genet). No placeholder mechanisms detected. Score 4 rather than 5 because abstract-only verifications could be upgraded to full-text for downstream-hub edges.

phenotype_audit.justification: Tiller_Number has 8 inputs (5 activators: MOC1, DLT, PIN9, LAX1, FON1; 3 inhibitors: TB1, PIN1B, ABA) - exceeds the 3-5 target and the 7-cap. All major effector classes represented: integrator TF (TB1), meristem TFs (MOC1, DLT, FON1), transporters (PIN1B, PIN9, LAX1), hormone (ABA direct). BUT missing a canonical direct Gibberellin arm (GA->SLR1->MOC1 routes indirectly) - defensible. Missing OsGATA8->TN direct (E150) and MOC3->TN direct (E154) - but MOC3 routes via FON1, and GATA8 routes via TCP19->DLT - defensible cascade routes. Score 3 because of over-count (8 > 7). Recommendation: consider whether PIN1B and PIN9 both being in the phenotype set is double-counting auxin-transport effects.

rejected_edges_review.justification: 75 curated edges not in the network. Categorised: (a) SUMMARY edges correctly excluded because cascade is present - majority, e.g. Strigolactone->TN (E100), Cytokinin->TN (E047), D53->TN (E129), IPA1->TN (E101), GATA8->TN (E150), PRR1->TN (E125), SLR1->TN (E105), Gibberellin->TN (E106), TAD1->TN (E127), TE->TN (E128), MOC3->TN (E154), OsPIN1a/b->TN (E057, E058), CCA1->TN (E126), CKX2->TN (E130), Sucrose->TN (E073), TAB1->TN (E079), miR444a->TN (E111), OsDRM1->TN (E163), Auxin->TN (E110), OsNiR->TN (E096), LAX2->TN (E077), NPF7.7->TN (E102), OsPIN2->TN (E059) - all defensible as cascade routes are represented; (b) BYPASS edges correctly excluded - Strigolactone->CKX9 (E056) [cascade via D53], Strigolactone->TB1 (E112) [cascade via D53->IPA1], Strigolactone->BZR1 (E086) [cascade via D53 possible], Strigolactone->PIN1 (E108) [no PIN1 in network]; (c) SIGNIFICANT non-summary edges silently excluded - GID1->SLR1 (E135), GID1->NGR5 (E136) [TRAP 3], OsTIR1->OsIAA (E064), OsIAA->OsARF (E065) [auxin signalling], TAB1->OSH1 (E080) [meristem], FON2->TAB1 (E081), D53->TPL (E140), TPL->IPA1 (E141), NCED1->ABA (E091), OsHOX12->NCED1 (E158), Strigolactone->OsHOX12 (E157), OsHK3->AHP (E160), IPA1->LOG (E010), IPA1->HK4 (E012), D14->D3 (E143), ABA->D10 (E159), Nitrogen->TB1 (E113), Nitrogen->TCP19 (E114). The category (c) silent exclusions drive the suggestions list.

key_player_density.justification: Per-node coverage ratios computed after normalising curated Os-prefix to network aliases (OsTB1->TB1, OsCCA1->CCA1, etc.). Key-player ratios: TB1 9/16=0.56, IPA1 10/16=0.62, Strigolactone 7/14=0.50, Cytokinin 8/14=0.57, Auxin 7/9=0.78, Nitrogen 6/10=0.60, MOC1 7/8=0.88, CCA1 8/9=0.89, D53 7/9=0.78, Sucrose 6/7=0.86, NGR5 8/9=0.89. Tiller_Number at 8/31=0.26 is visually alarming but the 23 excluded edges are 90%+ summary edges that have documented cascade routes (see rejected_edges_review). ABA at 1/4=0.25 has only 4 curated edges so falls below the 5-edge §9.11 threshold - but STILL signals a weak biosynthesis arm that deserves attention. No node with curated_count>=5 has ratio<0.30 after summary-edge correction. Score 4 rather than 5 because ABA biosynthesis arm is under-specified even if §9.11 formal threshold is not triggered.

per_node_audit.comment: Source node with zero activators/inhibitors despite NCED1->ABA (E091) being curated. Single output OK for a peripheral hormone arm, but biosynthesis depth missing.
Single activator where OsHK3 redundancy (E160) is documented. Could be composited as OsHK family or add OsHK3 as second activator.
Source node, one canonical downstream target. OK.

Auxin has 4 direct outgoing edges but no receptor in network. This is a Perception Gate (Motif 1) TRAP 3 - tir1+IAA rescue tests will fail. Either add OsTIR1+OsIAA+OsARF cascade, or document as framework limitation.
Single activator + single repressor-output is fine for a TF. Could add SL->BZR1 via D53 cascade if D53-BZR1 complex is modelled.
Source hormone with 2 clean outputs. Perception Gate not needed because BR receptor not in curated pool.
Excellent multi-output clock TF. All curated outputs included (N050-N054). Reciprocal PRR1 feedback correctly modelled.
CK degradation enzyme, DST-driven per Li 2013.

curated_regulators_excluded: Strigolactone(+) via E056 - summary, cascade via D53 is in-network
per_node_audit.comment: Properly gated via D53 (not direct SL->CKX9 bypass). Good Perception-Gate application.

Cytokinin has direct edge Cytokinin->TB1(-1) that bypasses HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1->TB1 cascade, which IS in the network. This is TRAP 3. Recommend remove Cytokinin->TB1 direct and let cascade carry the signal. Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA direct also bypasses HK4 signalling.
SL biosynthesis gene, environmental/hormonal inputs well-captured. ABA repression would add ABA-SL antagonism arm.

curated_regulators_excluded: TB1(+) via E024 - feedback, TRAP 1 risk
per_node_audit.comment: SL receptor. TB1->D14 positive feedback correctly excluded (would create TB1->D14->D53->IPA1->TB1 amplification loop).
SL biosynthesis enzyme (OsHTD1). Missing CCA1 direct? Not in curated for D17 so not a gap.
SL biosynthesis. Missing Auxin(+) input? Not curated as direct edge for D27, unlike D10/D17. OK.
F-box protein, source node with co-inhibitor role in Perception Gate. E143 D14->D3 is complex-formation, not necessary to model because D3+D14 already co-inhibit D53 in the gate motif.

curated_regulators_excluded: TPL(+) via E140 - could improve mechanism annotation
per_node_audit.comment: Central Perception-Gate target with 3-arm output. Missing TPL co-repressor is a mechanism-depth issue, not functionally critical for algebraic model.
BR effector, repressed by TCP19 - good cross-talk motif.
Zinc-finger TF, source node. Single target OK.
Source node. One of the newly-added Step 1.5 nodes - BUILDER placed it sensibly.
Environment source, canonical apical-dominance release mechanism.
CLV1-like receptor, part of meristem-size control.

curated_regulators_excluded: TAB1(-) via E081 - TAB1 not in network
per_node_audit.comment: CLV3-like peptide. Excluded edge to TAB1 only relevant if TAB1 is added.
GA biosynthesis enzyme, source node.
N-response TF, single target via TCP19 is core mechanism (Wang 2024).
Gibberellin directly inhibits SLR1 and NGR5 without GID1 receptor. TRAP 3 Perception-Gate violation - gid1 mutant + GA rescue will fail. HIGH priority add GID1 node and route GA through it.
CK receptor. Missing IPA1->HK4 (E012) would add transcriptional upregulation loop.

curated_regulators_excluded: miR529(-) via E014 - paralog of miR156
per_node_audit.comment: Master integrator of SL/clock/N/sugar signalling. Well-specified with 7 regulators. miR529 paralog missing but minor (redundant with miR156). IPA1->LOG (E010) and IPA1->HK4 (E012) outputs excluded - these add downstream CK-activation arm.
E3 ligase for IPA1, is_source=True correctly (no curated upstream in OA literature per literature_judge_report G011).
CK biosynthesis, auxin-antagonistic regulation. Missing IPA1->LOG (E010) would strengthen CK-biosynthesis hub, but LOG handles acute activation.

curated_regulators_excluded: LAX2(+) via E078 - LAX2 not in network
per_node_audit.comment: Axillary meristem initiation. LAX2 partner not in network - could be added as composite with LAX1. Ratio 0.40 (2/5) is borderline.
N-response repressor of TCP19. Clean motif.
Is_source=True but IPA1->LOG (E010) is curated. Moving LOG downstream of IPA1 would couple meristem-enlargement to CK activation.
Environmental source.
Excluded TB1->MADS57 feedback defensible (would close TB1->MADS57->D14->D53->IPA1->TB1 loop).
Generic MAX1 family collapsed node, complementing MAX1_1900 for N-specificity. Documented in literature_judge_report G013.
Os1900-specific N-responsive paralog (Ito 2023). Good separation from generic MAX1.

curated_regulators_excluded: OSH1(+) via E028 - OSH1 not in network
per_node_audit.comment: Central meristem-formation GRAS TF. All canonical regulators included. Motif-consistent (degradation-by-APC/C via TAD1, TE).
Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase, source node. Metabolic Sucrose producer.

curated_regulators_excluded: Tiller_Number direct via E154 - summary, cascade via FON1 is present
per_node_audit.comment: Axillary-meristem TF (TAB1/OsWUS ortholog). Wait - is MOC3 = TAB1? Literature says MOC3/TAB1/OsWUS are same; check node_annotations for clarity.

curated_regulators_excluded: GID1(-) via E136 - proper GA-perception gate
per_node_audit.comment: Critical N-response PRC2 recruiter (Wu 2020). 5-input hub well-specified. Missing edges NGR5->D10(-) and NGR5->D27(-) for PRC2-mediated SL-biosynthesis repression - not found in curated set; file as literature_gap if mechanism is needed.
Nitrate transceptor, source. E081 connection to NGR5 in-network.
Environmental source with 6 clean outputs - well-connected. Excluded OsNLP4->OsNiR arm is a separate N-assimilation pathway; defensible exclusion as peripheral.
Implicit OsPIN1a+1b composite (E011 and E058 land here). Composite not documented in node_annotations - should be.
NH4+-inducible transporter. Clean peripheral module.
Clock component, reciprocal feedback with CCA1 captured.
Environmental source, canonical Pi-starvation SL-induction arm.
Environmental source.
B-type RR in CK-signalling transduction chain. Single input OK for a phosphorelay output.

Currently Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA(+) is a bypass of the HK4->AHP cascade. Biologically correct edge would be AHP->RR_TYPEA(+) (phosphorelay). RR_TYPEA->Cytokinin(-1) is also a summary edge - the correct mechanism is RR_TYPEA repressing CK SIGNALLING at the RR21/AHP level, not CK levels. Consider restructuring.
Source node per literature_judge_report G011. Clean repressor of IPA1.
SLR1 (rice DELLA) directly inhibited by GA without GID1 gate. Must fix alongside Gibberellin bypass - add GID1 as intermediate, route GA through GID1 co-inhibition motif.

Excellent Perception-Gate purity: one outgoing edge to D14 receptor only. 6 biosynthetic inputs appropriately comprehensive. The OsHOX12->NCED1->ABA arm (E157, E158, E091) would add ABA biosynthesis depth - MEDIUM priority.

Sugar signalling hub with 4 outputs. Coverage ratio 0.86 (6/7). Good multi-output. Sucrose->D53(+) is counter-intuitive to 'sugars antagonize SL' but matches curated evidence (stabilisation of D53 protein).
Z-ISO, carotenoid precursor enzyme. Provides both SL and ABA biosynthesis inputs - clean shared precursor. Multi-Output Scaffold applied correctly.
Trehalose-6-P sugar signal. Clean Sucrose->T6P->TB1(-) arm.
APC/C component, source node per G011. Degrades MOC1.
Auxin biosynthesis, source node.

curated_regulators_excluded: Nitrogen(-) via E113 - N->TB1 direct per Wu 2020
per_node_audit.comment: 8 inputs exceeds the 7-cap. All curated regulators justified except possibly Auxin(+) which should route through OsTIR1/OsARF. Consider: (a) remove Cytokinin->TB1 direct (bypass), (b) N->TB1 direct could be added for Feed-Forward Loop with NGR5 (Wu 2020 specifically describes this). Balance: 3 activators / 5 inhibitors is appropriate for a tillering-repressor TF.

curated_regulators_excluded: Nitrogen(-) via E114 - direct - E113/114 both cover Feed-Forward motif
per_node_audit.comment: N-responsive TCP. LBD37/GATA8 cover the N-pathway inputs adequately. Direct N->TCP19 summary edge correctly excluded.
APC/C E3, source per G011. MOC1 degradation.

curated_regulators_excluded: 23+ summary edges - defensibly excluded as cascades present
per_node_audit.comment: 8 inputs exceeds 7-cap. Consider: (a) PIN1B and PIN9 both representing polar auxin transport - keep both (opposite signs), (b) FON1 redundant with MOC1 (MOC1 already activates FON1 indirectly via MOC3)? Actually FON1 routes: MOC1->MOC3->FON1->TN. So MOC1->TN and FON1->TN double-count. Consider removing MOC1->TN direct (summary) and let cascade carry, OR remove FON1->TN as an additional meristem arm. 5 activators / 3 inhibitors balance OK.
Chromatin factor, source. Represses TB1 via PRC2 chromatin remodelling (Yoon 2019).
Auxin biosynthesis enzyme family, source.
miRNA source. miR529 paralog not in network - could be composite.
miRNA source, canonical MADS57 regulator.

per_pathway_audit.comment: Six SL-biosynthesis enzyme nodes (D27, D17, D10, MAX1, MAX1_1900, T20) all have outgoing edges to Strigolactone. Environmental regulation (Pi, N) captured. Auxin regulation on D10/D17 captured. MAX1 paralog strategy (generic + Os1900-specific) is documented and sound.
missing_pieces: LBO rice ortholog (literature_judge_report G012 - unclosable)
NGR5->D10/D27 PRC2 repression arm (not in curated)
ABA->D10 repression (E159, curated but excluded)

per_pathway_audit.comment: Textbook Perception Gate (Motif 1) implementation. Strigolactone has exactly one outgoing edge (D14 receptor). D14 and D3 both co-inhibit D53 (-1 each). SL-mutant + GR24 rescue tests will behave correctly because D14-KO will prevent GR24 signal propagation.
missing_pieces: D14->D3 complex formation (E143) - biochemically real but functionally redundant with co-inhibition motif

per_pathway_audit.comment: D53 represses TB1 via D53->IPA1(-1) then IPA1->TB1(+1) cascade, which gives the correct sign but routes through IPA1. Song 2017 also documented direct D53-TB1 physical interaction - not found in curated_edges set difference, so likely not curated. Indirect cascade is acceptable if Song 2017 direct interaction is peripheral.
missing_pieces: Direct D53->TB1 physical-interaction edge if Song 2017 is re-mined in Step 1 - file as literature_gap

per_pathway_audit.pathway: D53 -> IPA1 direct and IPA1 -> TB1 activation
per_pathway_audit.comment: Both canonical edges present: D53->IPA1(-1) per Song 2017/Liu 2017 and IPA1->TB1(+1) per Lu 2013. The IPA1->D53(+1) feedback (D53 E136 via IPA1 activating its own repressor) also captured - sophisticated motif.
missing_pieces: TPL co-repressor (E140, E141) for mechanism depth

per_pathway_audit.comment: miR156->IPA1(-1) present. miR529 paralog missing (E014) but redundant.
missing_pieces: miR529 as composite with miR156 or separate regulator

per_pathway_audit.comment: MOC1 has 4 inputs (D53-, TAD1-, TE-, SLR1+) and 3 outputs (TN+, LAX1+, MOC3+). All canonical MOC1 regulators captured. APC/C-mediated degradation via TAD1 and TE present (Motif 4 biosynthesis-degradation pattern applied).
missing_pieces: OSH1 (E028) downstream of MOC1 - OSH1 not in network

per_pathway_audit.comment: Both TAD1->MOC1(-1) and TE->MOC1(-1) present. APC/C complex collapsed into its two relevant subunits - defensible composite.

per_pathway_audit.pathway: OsLAX1/OsLAX2 -> axillary meristem initiation (upstream of MOC1)
per_pathway_audit.comment: LAX1 is in network but positioned DOWNSTREAM of MOC1 (MOC1->LAX1+). Biologically, LAX1/LAX2 are upstream of MOC1 in meristem INITIATION (Oikawa 2009). Consider whether this is a directionality issue - actual biology is both LAX1 and MOC1 act on axillary meristem formation in parallel, with LAX1 upstream for initiation and MOC1 for outgrowth. Current topology (MOC1->LAX1) implies MOC1 activates LAX1, which may be correct for outgrowth phase but misses the initiation role.
missing_pieces: LAX2 as LAX1 partner (E077, E078)
LAX1-upstream regulators for initiation phase

per_pathway_audit.comment: TB1->Tiller_Number(-1) sign correct. 8 input regulators cover SL (via IPA1), clock (CCA1), hormones (CK, Auxin, BR, T6P), chromatin (VIL2), N (NGR5). Slight overloading (8 > 7-cap).
missing_pieces: Nitrogen direct input (E113) for FFL with NGR5 - but this conflicts with 7-cap; defensible exclusion

per_pathway_audit.comment: TAR2 and YUCCA both source nodes feeding Auxin(+). Clean biosynthesis. No TRP-TAR-YUCCA intermediate needed at this abstraction level.
PIN1B and PIN9 in network. PIN2 (E059 OsPIN2->TN+1) missing, but opposite-signed from PIN1a/1b so possibly redundant directionality. PIN1B is implicit OsPIN1a+1b composite - not documented.
missing_pieces: PIN2 as separate node or composite
Document PIN1B as composite in node_annotations

per_pathway_audit.comment: ENTIRE Auxin signalling cascade absent. OsTIR1 (E063, E064), OsIAA (E064, E065), OsARF (E065) all missing. Auxin has 4 direct outgoing edges (D10, D17, IPT, TB1) that are ARF-downstream summary edges. TRAP 3 Perception-Gate bypass for all 4 targets. tir1 mutant + IAA rescue tests will FAIL.
missing_pieces: OsTIR1 receptor node (E063, E064)
OsIAA and OsARF TFs (E064, E065)
Restructure Auxin direct edges to route through TIR1-IAA-ARF

per_pathway_audit.pathway: Cytokinin biosynthesis (IPT + LOG) vs degradation (CKX2, CKX9)
per_pathway_audit.comment: Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance (Motif 4) correctly applied. IPT (synthesise) + LOG (activate) as activators; CKX2 and CKX9 as degradation arms. OsCKX4 and OsCKX11 paralogs missing - could be composites.
missing_pieces: OsCKX4 and OsCKX11 paralog handling (E044, E046)

per_pathway_audit.pathway: CK signalling (OsHK -> OsHP -> OsRR-B -> target + type-A feedback)
per_pathway_audit.comment: Cytokinin -> HK4 -> AHP -> RR21 -> IPA1 cascade present and correctly layered. RR_TYPEA negative feedback present but wired as Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA (bypass) rather than AHP->RR_TYPEA. Critical issue: Cytokinin->TB1(-1) direct bypass coexists with the proper cascade, meaning CK treatment will hit TB1 directly even when HK4 is KO'd. hk4 + trans-zeatin rescue test will fail.
missing_pieces: Remove Cytokinin->TB1 direct bypass
Rewire RR_TYPEA as AHP->RR_TYPEA(+)
OsHK3 paralog composite with HK4 (E160, E161)

per_pathway_audit.pathway: Nitrogen signalling (NRT1.1B -> NGR5 -> PRC2 on SL biosynthesis)
per_pathway_audit.comment: NRT1.1B->NGR5(+1) present. NGR5 represses TB1/D14/IPA1 (all tillering-repressor arms, so NET pro-tillering). BUT: Wu 2020 Science specifically documents NGR5 PRC2-mediated repression of SL biosynthesis genes (D10, D27) - this would be NGR5->D10(-), NGR5->D27(-). Not found in curated set, so file as literature_gap or request re-mining of Wu 2020. Also E113 Nitrogen->TB1 direct curated but excluded - defensible because NGR5 already carries N signal to TB1.
missing_pieces: NGR5->D10(-1), NGR5->D27(-1) PRC2 repression arm - literature_gap (check Wu 2020 re-mining)
E113 Nitrogen->TB1 direct is redundant

per_pathway_audit.comment: TCP19 has 2 inputs (LBD37-, GATA8-) and 1 output (DLT-). Multi-input N-integration hub. Zhang 2021 and Wang 2024 canonical arms both captured.

per_pathway_audit.pathway: GA / DELLA signalling (OsGA20ox -> GA -> OsGID1 -> (degrade) SLR1)
per_pathway_audit.comment: GA20OX2->Gibberellin->SLR1(-1) is DIRECT without GID1 receptor. Same for Gibberellin->NGR5(-1). TRAP 3 Perception-Gate bypass. gid1 + GA rescue tests will fail. HIGH priority add GID1 node and route GA through GID1 co-inhibition motif on SLR1 and NGR5 (using E135, E136).
missing_pieces: GID1 receptor node
GID1->SLR1(-1) and GID1->NGR5(-1) co-inhibition edges (E135, E136)
Remove direct Gibberellin->SLR1 and Gibberellin->NGR5 (become bypass after GID1 added)

per_pathway_audit.comment: Auxin->IPT(-1) captures classical apical-auxin-represses-CK-biosynthesis. Decapitation->Auxin(-1) captures decapitation-induced CK accumulation indirectly via IPT derepression. CK then acts via HK4-IPA1-TB1 cascade (and direct bypass, which is the issue). Overall biology correctly represented.
Sucrose->T6P(+1)->TB1(-1) captures T6P-mediated bud outgrowth. Sucrose also -> CCA1(-), -> D53(+), -> NGR5(+). Multi-output hub well-done. Coverage ratio 0.86.

TAB1 (= OsWUS) is the rice meristem-initiation ortholog of Arabidopsis WUSCHEL. Curated edges E079 TAB1->TN(+1), E080 TAB1->OSH1, E081 FON2->TAB1(-1). ALL EXCLUDED. MOC3 in network may be TAB1 alias - check node_annotations; if so, this is a naming issue not a missing-node issue. If MOC3 != TAB1, the canonical meristem-initiation arm is missing.
missing_pieces: TAB1 node or MOC3=TAB1 alias documentation
TAB1->OSH1 (E080) and TAB1->TN (E079)

per_pathway_audit.comment: ABA is in network but as a near-source (input: T20 only from E156 summary). Missing: NCED1 (rate-limiting ABA biosynthesis enzyme, E091), OsHOX12 (direct NCED1 activator, E158), Strigolactone->OsHOX12 (E157) cascade. ABA coverage ratio 0.25. Adding this arm would create SL -> OsHOX12 -> NCED1 -> ABA -> TN (-) - a parallel SL-to-TN path.
missing_pieces: NCED1 node (E091)
OsHOX12 node (E157, E158)
ABA biosynthesis cascade

suggestions.description: Add GA Perception Gate: introduce GID1 node and route Gibberellin through GID1 co-inhibition motif on SLR1 and NGR5
suggestions.biological_justification: Gibberellin currently has 2 direct outgoing edges (SLR1-, NGR5-) with no GID1 receptor, violating Motif 1 Perception Gate purity. GA-GID1 complex is the canonical mechanism for SLR1 and NGR5 degradation (Ueguchi-Tanaka 2005; Wu 2020 Science). Without GID1, gid1 mutant + GA rescue tests will incorrectly predict GA-responsive rescue.
suggestions.implementation: Add GID1 as new GENE node (type: GENE, is_source: false). Add edges Gibberellin->GID1(+1) [mechanism: GA binds GID1 receptor forming GA-GID1 complex] - file as literature_gap if no direct curated edge for this activation, otherwise use E135 endpoint; then convert existing Gibberellin->SLR1(-1) to Gibberellin->GID1(+1) plus GID1->SLR1(-1) using E135 and GID1->NGR5(-1) using E136 (co-inhibition with the already-present Gibberellin edges converted to GID1 intermediates).

suggestions.description: Remove Cytokinin->TB1 direct bypass edge (TRAP 3 Perception-Gate violation); let existing HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1->TB1 cascade carry the signal
suggestions.biological_justification: Cytokinin currently has Cytokinin->TB1(-1) direct AND the full HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1->TB1 cascade is in the network (RR21 activates IPA1 which activates TB1). CK represses TB1 ONLY through signalling (HK4-mediated), not CK-level-to-TB1 direct. The direct edge will incorrectly allow exogenous CK to repress TB1 even in hk4-KO backgrounds (bypass violation).
suggestions.implementation: Remove Cytokinin->TB1(-1) edge from network.json and algebraic_equations.json. Verify IPA1->TB1(+1) cascade carries the correct signalling sign: CK->HK4(+)->AHP(+)->RR21(+)->IPA1(+)->TB1(+) gives CK->TB1 NET POSITIVE. But curated mechanism (Cytokinin REPRESSES TB1) requires NET NEGATIVE. This suggests the cascade carries OPPOSITE sign - probably the actual mechanism is CK promotes tillering by REPRESSING TB1 via a negative node. Re-examine the sign of RR21->IPA1 (currently +1) - if curated shows type-B RR ACTIVATES IPA1, the cascade gives CK->TB1 (+). The CORRECT cascade is CK->HK4->AHP->type-A-RR(-)->IPA1 or CK signalling represses IPA1. Re-verify against Doi 2007/Ashikari 2005. If cascade cannot carry correct sign, keep Cytokinin->TB1 direct but document as framework limitation.

suggestions.description: Add OsTIR1 auxin receptor to establish Auxin Perception Gate
suggestions.biological_justification: Auxin currently has 4 direct outgoing edges (D10+, D17+, IPT-, TB1+) with NO receptor in the network. tir1 or OsAFB mutant + IAA rescue tests will incorrectly predict auxin responsiveness. Canonical Auxin signalling is Auxin-TIR1/AFB-SCF complex ubiquitinates OsIAA repressors releasing OsARFs, which then regulate transcription of auxin-responsive genes. At minimum, the Auxin->TB1 summary edge should route through TIR1.
suggestions.implementation: Add OsTIR1 node (type: GENE, is_source: true). Add edge Auxin->OsTIR1(+1) [E063 mechanism: auxin binding]. Optionally add OsIAA and OsARF nodes for full cascade (E064, E065). Restructure Auxin->TB1(+1) direct edge to route via OsARF. If minimal fix: add OsTIR1->TB1(-1) summary (since TIR1-auxin promotes degradation of IAA repressors, net effect on TB1 depends on OsARF sign).

suggestions.description: Fix TB1 overload (8 inputs > 7-cap): remove Cytokinin->TB1 direct (covered by S002) and reconsider Auxin->TB1 direct vs routing through TIR1 (covered by S003)
suggestions.biological_justification: TB1 has 8 inputs, exceeding BUILDER §1.1 hard cap of 7. Two of those 8 are Perception-Gate bypass edges that should be removed: Cytokinin->TB1 (S002) and Auxin->TB1 (S003). Removing both brings TB1 to 6 inputs and resolves both overload and bypass violations simultaneously.
suggestions.implementation: After applying S002 and S003, TB1 will have 6 inputs (IPA1+, NGR5-, CCA1+, BZR1-, T6P-, VIL2-). Verify this matches the 1-5 target range with 6 being acceptable. If still too many, consider collapsing BZR1 and VIL2 as chromatin-repressor composite.

suggestions.description: Fix Tiller_Number overload (8 inputs > 7-cap): consider removing MOC1->TN direct (cascade via MOC3->FON1->TN already present) OR removing FON1->TN
suggestions.biological_justification: Tiller_Number has 8 inputs (5 act / 3 inh) exceeding 7-cap. MOC1->TN(+1) and FON1->TN(+1) are partially redundant: MOC1->MOC3->FON1->TN is already a complete cascade. Biologically MOC1 affects tillering both via MOC3/FON1 (meristem size) AND direct bud formation. Pick one canonical route.
suggestions.implementation: Option A (recommended): Remove MOC1->Tiller_Number(+1) direct and let MOC1->MOC3->FON1->TN cascade carry. Option B: Remove FON1->Tiller_Number(+1) and keep MOC1 direct. Option A preferred because it preserves the meristem-size-regulation motif (MOC3/FON1 are meristem-specific; MOC1 is a broader regulator).

suggestions.description: Add ABA biosynthesis cascade: OsHOX12 -> NCED1 -> ABA, with upstream Strigolactone -> OsHOX12
suggestions.biological_justification: ABA is in network as a near-source (only input: T20 summary). Curated edges E091 (NCED1->ABA), E158 (OsHOX12->NCED1), E157 (SL->OsHOX12) form a proper biosynthesis arm. Adding this arm creates a second SL->TN negative path (SL->OsHOX12->NCED1->ABA->TN(-)) parallel to the D14-D53-IPA1-TB1 arm, improving SL-signalling model robustness. literature_judge_report §8 specifically lists these as unplaced nodes.
suggestions.implementation: Add NCED1 (GENE, is_source: false) and OsHOX12 (GENE, is_source: false) as new nodes. Add edges Strigolactone->OsHOX12(+1) [E157], OsHOX12->NCED1(+1) [E158], NCED1->ABA(+1) [E091]. Check Perception-Gate: Strigolactone would then have 2 outgoing edges (D14 and OsHOX12), which breaks the pure gate. Compromise: route OsHOX12 via D53 (D53->OsHOX12(-1))? File as literature_gap if D53->OsHOX12 is not curated.

suggestions.description: Add TAB1 (OsWUS) axillary meristem initiation node with FON2 repression and TN activation
suggestions.biological_justification: TAB1/OsWUS is the rice ortholog of Arabidopsis WUSCHEL, required for axillary meristem initiation (Tanaka 2015). Curated edges E079 TAB1->TN(+1), E081 FON2->TAB1(-1) both exist. Currently MOC3 is in the network and may be aliased to TAB1 - VERIFY node_annotations; if MOC3 = TAB1, this is a naming ambiguity issue (rename for clarity). If MOC3 != TAB1, add TAB1 as separate node.
suggestions.implementation: First check node_annotations.json: does MOC3 annotation state it is TAB1/OsWUS ortholog? If yes: rename MOC3 to TAB1 throughout, preserving existing edges. If no: add TAB1 as new node with FON2->TAB1(-1), TAB1->Tiller_Number(+1) edges. Note: MOC3->FON1 edge should be preserved either way.

suggestions.description: Document PIN1B as explicit OsPIN1a+1b composite node in node_annotations.json
suggestions.biological_justification: Curated edges E011 (OsPIN1b) and E058 (OsPIN1b) both map to PIN1B in the network; E057 (OsPIN1a->TN) is not mapped. This is an implicit composite that should be documented to explain the single PIN1B node rather than separate PIN1a/PIN1b.
suggestions.proposed_mechanism: PIN1B represents OsPIN1a+OsPIN1b composite auxin-efflux carrier family; IPA1 directly binds OsPIN1b promoter to activate auxin efflux (E011), and loss of OsPIN1a or OsPIN1b individually increases tiller number (E057, E058).
suggestions.implementation: Update node_annotations.json for PIN1B to include 'composite_members: [OsPIN1a, OsPIN1b]' and list both curated DOIs.

suggestions.description: Rewire Cytokinin -> RR_TYPEA direct edge to AHP -> RR_TYPEA for cascade consistency
suggestions.biological_justification: RR_TYPEA (type-A response regulators) are induced by CK signalling, not by CK ligand directly. The canonical two-component signalling chain is CK->HK->AHP->type-A_RR (transcriptional induction). Current Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA(+1) bypasses the phosphorelay and creates the same class of bypass as Cytokinin->TB1 (S002).
suggestions.implementation: Remove Cytokinin->RR_TYPEA(+1) direct. Add AHP->RR_TYPEA(+1) with evidence that CK induces type-A RRs via signalling (E138 Tsai 2012). Verify RR_TYPEA->Cytokinin(-1) is actually meant to be RR_TYPEA->AHP(-1) or RR_TYPEA->HK4(-1) negative feedback on signalling (not on CK levels).

suggestions.description: Add IPA1 -> LOG (+1) and IPA1 -> HK4 (+1) to create IPA1-CK feed-forward loop
suggestions.biological_justification: IPA1 directly activates OsLOG (E010 cytokinin-activating enzyme) and OsHK4 (E012 CK receptor). This creates a feed-forward motif where IPA1 promotes BOTH direct tillering (via TB1 - but wait, IPA1->TB1 is positive, which REPRESSES tillering) AND CK signalling. Actually biology: IPA1 GOF (ipa1-1D) REDUCES tillers (via TB1 activation) but promotes panicle development. Adding IPA1->LOG/HK4 captures panicle-CK arm.
suggestions.implementation: Add edges IPA1->LOG(+1) [E010] and IPA1->HK4(+1) [E012]. Verify LOG node is converted from is_source: true to is_source: false after adding activator.

suggestions.description: Consider CKX family composite: merge CKX2, CKX9, OsCKX4 paralogs into OsCKX_family composite OR add separate CKX4/11 nodes
suggestions.biological_justification: OsCKX family has 11 paralogs; curated set covers CKX2 (E043, N066), CKX9 (E045, N033), CKX4 (E044), CKX11 (E046). All degrade cytokinin. Current network has CKX2 and CKX9 separate. Triple-redundancy modelling concerns (TRAP 2) if one mutant is silenced.
suggestions.implementation: Option A: Keep CKX2 and CKX9 separate (current), add CKX4 and CKX11 as new nodes for complete family. Option B: Merge into OsCKX_family composite with all four paralogs. Option A preferred because DST->CKX2 (E066 promoter specificity) and D53->CKX9 (N033 SL-gated) are MECHANISTICALLY DISTINCT arms - merging loses specificity.

suggestions.description: Add TPL co-repressor to document D53->IPA1 mechanism depth
suggestions.biological_justification: D53 represses IPA1 via recruitment of TOPLESS (TPL) co-repressor and EAR-motif-mediated transcriptional repression. Adding TPL as intermediate node clarifies mechanism and allows TPL-specific mutants (tpl-KO) to be modelled.
suggestions.proposed_mechanism: D53 contains EAR motifs and recruits TOPLESS/TPR co-repressors to IPA1 chromatin loci for transcriptional silencing
suggestions.implementation: Add TPL node (GENE, is_source: true). Add D53->TPL(+1) [E140] and TPL->IPA1(-1) [E141]. Optionally remove direct D53->IPA1(-1) to force cascade, or keep both for redundancy.

suggestions.description: Add ABA -> D10 (-1) inhibition of SL biosynthesis for ABA-SL crosstalk
suggestions.biological_justification: ABA represses SL biosynthesis genes (D10, D17, D27) per E159. This adds an ABA-SL antagonism arm: ABA->D10(-)->SL reduces SL production when ABA is high. Complements the direct ABA->TN(-) edge with a SL-mediated parallel path.
suggestions.implementation: Add ABA->D10(-1) edge with E159 evidence. Note: this creates ABA having 2 outgoing edges (TN- and D10-) which is a Multi-Output Scaffold - acceptable.

suggestions.description: Add miR529 -> IPA1 (-1) as redundant miR156-family IPA1 regulator
suggestions.biological_justification: miR529a cleaves OsSPL14/IPA1 mRNA at the same site as miR156 (E014). Adding miR529 as separate miRNA source would model miR156/miR529 redundancy for IPA1 silencing.
suggestions.implementation: Add miR529 node (REGULATORY_RNA, is_source: true). Add miR529->IPA1(-1) [E014]. Alternative: composite miR156+miR529 into single miR156-family node (cleaner if both are redundant).

suggestions.description: Request re-mining Wu 2020 Science for explicit NGR5 -> D10/D27 PRC2-H3K27me3 repression edges
suggestions.biological_justification: Wu 2020 Science (doi: 10.1126/science.aaz2046) documents NGR5 promoting tillering by PRC2-H3K27me3 repression of SL-biosynthesis genes (not present in current curated set per the rejected edges audit). These edges would CLOSE the N->NGR5->SL-biosynthesis feedback loop and explain why NGR5 promotes tillering despite being SL-pathway-adjacent. Currently NGR5 represses TB1/D14/IPA1 directly (tillering-repressor arm) but does NOT connect to SL biosynthesis.
suggestions.implementation: Request Step 1 LITERATURE REVIEW to add edges NGR5->D10(-1) and NGR5->D27(-1) from Wu 2020 Section on PRC2 targets. If not curated-able, document as literature_gap G014 in next round's literature_judge_report.

suggestions.description: Flag missing D53 -> TB1 direct physical interaction edge (Song 2017)
suggestions.biological_justification: Song 2017 (Nat Plants 10.1038/nplants.2017.156) documented D53 directly binding both IPA1 AND OsTB1 via physical interaction. The D53->IPA1 arm is captured but D53->TB1 direct is not in curated_edges. Currently the D53->IPA1->TB1 cascade gives correct sign but routes through IPA1. Adding the direct D53-TB1 edge would add mechanism redundancy.
suggestions.implementation: Request Step 1 re-mining of Song 2017 for explicit D53-TB1 physical interaction figure (likely Fig 3 or similar). Flag as literature_gap if not findable.

literature_gaps: NGR5 -> D10/D27 PRC2-mediated H3K27me3 repression of SL biosynthesis genes (Wu 2020 Science 10.1126/science.aaz2046) - not in current curated_edges despite being a canonical Wu 2020 finding. Recommend Step 1 re-mining.

D53 -> OsTB1 direct physical interaction (Song 2017 Nat Plants 10.1038/nplants.2017.156) - Song 2017 documented D53 binds both IPA1 and TB1; IPA1 arm captured, TB1 direct arm is not curated. Cascade via IPA1 gives correct sign but misses the redundant direct interaction.
Detailed E3 ligase / upstream kinase cascade for IPI1, SHI1, DST, TAD1, TE, FON2 (listed in literature_judge_report G011 as unclosable with current OA literature). These remain is_source nodes.
LBO (LATERAL BRANCHING OXIDOREDUCTASE) rice ortholog - Arabidopsis LBO (Brewer 2016) catalyses MeCLA tailoring step in SL biosynthesis; rice ortholog not identified in current OA search (G012).
Rice PIN1 subcellular re-localisation by SL (Strigolactone -> PIN1 PM depletion, E108) would add auxin-transport-rerouting motif but PIN1 generic is not in network (only PIN1B composite).
